perFrameCode: // below parameters belong to an extended "Lotka-Volterra" model (simple predator-prey differential equation system - see wikipedia)
startx = 0.7; // initial predator population (used only for the phase plot)
a = 1.0; // LV-model parameters - some pairs reach a so-called limit-cycle
q5 = a; // the model parameters are synchronized for the phase plot wave and the per-vertex warp
// below parameters belong to a synchronized affine transformation for waves and the per-vertex code (rotate, scale, translate)
q26 = 0.5*x; // center for rotation and scaling (x)
q27 = 0.5*y; // center for rotation and scaling (y)
// TODO: compensate parameter changes for an invariant canvas pane via difference quotients ;)
// integration again (for constant change in one variable)
perPixelCode: // have a nice warp effect? - rotate it like a shape!
x = q26 + cos(-angle)*X1 -sin(-angle)*Y1; // rotate the x/y pane
dx1 = cos(angle)*dx - sin(angle)*dy; // and finally the dx/dy pane is rotated to match the x/y pane
